questions.question: What is the defining feature that distinguishes Bipolar I from Bipolar II disorder?

options.text: Bipolar I involves depression while Bipolar II does not
Bipolar I involves at least one full manic episode, while Bipolar II involves hypomanic episodes without full mania
Bipolar II is a more severe form of the disorder
Bipolar I only occurs in adults, while Bipolar II can occur at any age

questions.explanation: Bipolar I disorder requires at least one full manic episode (lasting 7+ days or requiring hospitalization), while Bipolar II involves hypomanic episodes (at least 4 days) that do not reach the severity or duration of full mania, combined with major depressive episodes.

questions.question: Why are mixed features in bipolar disorder particularly concerning?

options.text: They are the most common presentation of bipolar disorder
They only occur in people who stop taking medication
They carry a higher risk of suicide due to simultaneous hopelessness, agitation, and impulsivity
They indicate the person has been misdiagnosed

questions.explanation: Mixed features -- where manic and depressive symptoms occur simultaneously or in rapid alternation -- are among the most dangerous states in bipolar disorder. The combination of depressive hopelessness with manic energy, agitation, and impulsivity creates a significantly elevated risk of suicide.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most consistent trigger for mood episodes in bipolar disorder?

options.text: Eating unhealthy food
Watching too much television
Sleep disruption
Cold weather

questions.explanation: Sleep disruption is the most consistent trigger for mood episodes in bipolar disorder. Even one night of lost sleep can precipitate a manic episode. This is why sleep regulation is a cornerstone of bipolar self-management.

questions.question: What does the lesson identify as the single strongest predictor of mood stability in bipolar disorder?

options.text: Daily exercise
Medication adherence
Avoiding all stress
Having a large social network

questions.explanation: Medication adherence is the single strongest predictor of stability in bipolar disorder. Mood stabilizers and atypical antipsychotics reduce the frequency and severity of episodes, and stopping medication is the most common cause of relapse.

questions.question: How does Cyclothymic Disorder (Cyclothymia) differ from Bipolar I and Bipolar II?

options.text: It involves more severe manic episodes
It only lasts a few weeks
It involves chronic subthreshold mood fluctuations for at least 2 years that do not meet full criteria for hypomania or major depression
It is a temporary condition that resolves on its own

questions.explanation: Cyclothymic Disorder involves chronic fluctuating mood lasting at least 2 years (1 year in adolescents), with periods of hypomanic and depressive symptoms that do not meet the full criteria for a hypomanic episode or a major depressive episode. Symptoms are present at least half the time.
